name: NVIDIA H100 80GB SXM
notes: Hopper architecture. AI training/inference flagship. NVLink 4.0, 3.35 TB/s memory bandwidth.

name: NVIDIA A100 80GB SXM
notes: Ampere architecture. Widely available in cloud. 2.0 TB/s memory bandwidth. Industry workhorse.

name: NVIDIA A100 40GB PCIe
notes: PCIe form factor. 1.6 TB/s bandwidth. Good for single-GPU inference of mid-size models.

name: NVIDIA L40S
notes: Ada Lovelace datacenter GPU. 48GB GDDR6 with ECC. Cost-effective inference alternative to A100.

name: NVIDIA L4
notes: Ada Lovelace low-power inference GPU. 72W TDP. Ideal for edge and power-constrained deployments.

name: NVIDIA RTX 4090
notes: Ada Lovelace consumer flagship. 24GB GDDR6X, 1.0 TB/s bandwidth. Strong for local inference.

name: NVIDIA RTX 4080
notes: Ada Lovelace upper mid-range. 16GB limits model size. Good for 7B models in FP16.

name: NVIDIA RTX 3090
notes: Ampere consumer. 24GB good for 13B models in Int4. Widely available used market.

name: NVIDIA RTX 6000 Ada
notes: Ada Lovelace workstation. 48GB ECC GDDR6. Same GPU as L40S in workstation form factor.

name: NVIDIA A6000
notes: Ampere workstation GPU. 48GB ECC. Good for 30B models in Int4. Professional reliability.

name: NVIDIA RTX 5090
notes: Blackwell consumer flagship. 32GB GDDR7, 1.8 TB/s bandwidth. Best consumer GPU for local LLM inference.
